subject: History (Our Pasts - II)
chapter: Tracing Changes Through a Thousand Years

activities_projects.title: Old vs. New Map Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Understand how geographical records change over time
materials: Textbook images of Al-Idrisi and French cartographer maps
steps: Look at the map by Al-Idrisi (1154) and notice that South India is at the top
Look at the French cartographer's map (1720s) and see how it looks more familiar
Trace the coastline of India from both maps
Discuss why the second map was more useful for European sailors
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes that the science of cartography (map-making) changed significantly over centuries
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification of differences between the two maps (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Terminology Time Machine
activities_projects.objective: Identify how the meaning of words changes
steps: Read the section on the word 'Hindustan' and 'Foreigner'
Write down what 'Foreigner' means today (someone from another country)
Write down what 'Foreigner' meant in the medieval period (anyone not from that village)
Explain why a city-dweller might think of a forest-dweller as a foreigner
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Learns that historical terms must be understood in their specific time context
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct explanation of the changing meanings of 'Foreigner' and 'Hindustan' (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Scribe's Dilemma
activities_projects.objective: Experience how manual copying leads to changes in text
steps: Ask one student to copy a paragraph and make one tiny change in a word
Ask the next student to copy from the first student’s paper, and so on
Compare the last student's paper with the original text
Discuss how historians struggle when they don't have the original manuscript
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the difficulties created by the lack of printing presses in the past
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation in copying and identifying errors introduced (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: New Foods and Technologies Chart
activities_projects.objective: Identify things that arrived in India during this period
steps: List the new foods mentioned: Potatoes, Corn, Chillies, Tea, Coffee
Draw the Persian Wheel (used for irrigation) and the Spinning Wheel (weaving)
Note down that fire-arms (for combat) also appeared during this time
Label the chart as 'New Arrivals (700-1750)'
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the technological and food innovations of the medieval period
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and labeling of food and technology items (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Rise of Jatis Poster
activities_projects.objective: Understand social changes in the medieval period
steps: Write 'Jati' in the center
List how Jatis were formed (based on occupation and background)
Mention that Jatis had their own rules and 'Jati Panchayats'
Draw a small symbol for an occupation (e.g., a pot for a potter)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes the increasing complexity of Indian society into sub-castes
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the Jati system description (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Archives Visit Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Learn where historical documents are kept
steps: Pretend the classroom is a National Archive
Collect 'manuscripts' (student drawings) and store them safely
Write a rule for the archive: 'No touching without gloves' or 'Keep in a cool place'
Explain why we keep old papers in archives instead of throwing them away
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Defines an 'Archive' and understands its importance for historians
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct definition of an archive and its function (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Patron and Poet Roleplay
activities_projects.objective: Understand the change in religious and social support
steps: One student acts as a wealthy 'Patron' (a powerful ruler)
Another acts as a Brahmin scholar or an artist
Show the Patron giving money or land to the scholar to build a temple
Discuss why Brahmanas became very important during this period
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explains why certain social groups gained prestige through patronage
activities_projects.evaluation: Understanding of the term 'Patron' and its historical role (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Bhakti vs. Old Religion
steps: Write down one main feature of the 'Bhakti' idea (love for a personal god)
Note that this did not require priests or elaborate rituals
Mention that Islam also arrived in India through merchants and migrants
Identify the 'Quran' as the holy book of Muslims
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies major religious developments including Bhakti and the arrival of Islam
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct points regarding Bhakti and Islam (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Time-Period Timeline
activities_projects.objective: Visualize the broad time scale of the chapter
steps: Draw a line representing the years 700 to 1750
Mark the beginning as 'Medieval Period'
Mark the end of the period when the British arrived
Highlight that this thousand-year span saw huge changes in India
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops a sense of chronology for the medieval period in India
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the dates and period labels (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Historian's Tools
activities_projects.objective: List sources used to study the past
steps: Create cards for: Coins, Inscriptions, Architecture, and Textual Records
Mix them up and try to group them
Discuss why 'Textual Records' became much more common in this period (paper became cheap)
Write one sentence on which source is your favorite
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies the various sources historians use to reconstruct the medieval past
activities_projects.evaluation: Identification of sources and why paper changed record-keeping (10 marks)
